Easy RSH

Easy RSH is a compact C++17 remote shell project focused on readable systems code.
It demonstrates POSIX sockets, authenticated client-server sessions, command execution,
fork-per-client concurrency, and process management in a small codebase.

Primary site: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/
Repository: https://github.com/xsuneth/Easy-RSH

Main documentation:
- Quick start: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/docs
- Installation: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/docs/installation
- Server setup: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/docs/server-setup
- Client usage: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/docs/client-usage
- Architecture: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/docs/architecture
- Security: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/docs/security
- CLI reference: https://easy-rsh.vercel.app/docs/cli-reference

Notes:
- Intended for Linux and POSIX-style environments.
- Uses salted SHA-256 credentials and OpenSSL-backed randomness.
- Not positioned as a hardened remote administration service for hostile networks.
